description: Behavioral expectations for proving hook guidance changes agent behavior, not only that hooks emit text.

agent_must_do: acknowledge the unresolved live-hook workflow
avoid claiming delivery is complete
route to verification or user decision before final delivery
continue instead of producing a final delivery answer
fill missing goal-fit or evidence state
surface the exact blocker if the runtime blocks stop
treat strict Stop guidance as a blocker when the runtime exposes the adapter result
continue or report the exact stop gate instead of final delivery

guidance_must_include: CONTEXT MAP: use docs/context-map.md before broad repo rediscovery
agent_must_do: treat hook guidance as additional context when the runtime exposes it
avoid final delivery while critique or evidence is unresolved
use context-map recovery guidance before broad rediscovery

cases.description: After execute-plan completes in the Builder Kit build flow, the next gate is review-work critique before verify-work evidence. Review is report-only and cannot be treated as verification evidence. Open critique findings route back through execution before re-review and re-verify.
agent_must_do: delegate to review-work for report-only critique before verify-work
not count report-only critique as verification evidence
route open critique findings back through execute-plan, then re-review and re-verify

cases.description: A fresh coding/build prompt with no active sidecar should still steer into the builder kit's builder.build workflow before source edits. Claude Code must receive this as prompt-submit additional context through the hook adapter.

fixture_state.prompt: Please implement the new settings API and update its tests.

agent_must_do: use the `builder` kit's `builder.build` workflow before source edits or implementation commands
use deliver by default for coding/build work unless the user explicitly requested TDD
keep the session on builder.build and do not bypass plan-work -> execute-plan -> review-work -> verify-work
continue from local verification into publish, release readiness, and learning feedback
record NOT_VERIFIED for live influence until codex exec exposes project hook guidance as model context
continue using adapter and installed-command evidence for protocol coverage

cases.description: When a user sends 'let's build X' and the shape step gate is incomplete, the hook must steer the agent to explore the repo and collect shape evidence instead of implementing. Agent must ask one question at a time with a recommended answer. See Builder Kit shape flow step:shape.
agent_must_do: explore the repo (AGENTS.md, docs/) before asking the first question
ask for problem_statement before doing any implementation
ask one question at a time — not all shape fields at once
include a recommended answer with each question
not start implementing routing logic before shape gate is complete

cases.description: At the pull-work step, the agent must check the backlog (gh issue list --label ready) before asking the user which item to take. WIP conflicts must also be checked before confirming. See Builder Kit build flow step:pull-work.
agent_must_do: run `gh issue list --label ready` before asking user which issue to take
check git status and open PRs for WIP conflicts before confirming
record worktree_decision with justification based on risk level

cases.description: When Builder Kit build flow reaches planning without recorded pickup Probe decisions or accepted gaps, the hook must steer back to design-probe instead of allowing plan-work to proceed. Direct pull-work and plan-work remain valid when the user invokes those primitives outside Builder Kit build flow.
cases.user_message: Use Builder Kit build flow and start plan-work now.
agent_must_do: route decision_gap back to design-probe/pickup Probe before retrying plan-work
record Probe decisions or explicitly accepted gaps in the pull-work or plan handoff artifact
ask one alignment question at a time only if repo/provider context leaves a genuine decision gap
preserve direct pull-work and direct plan-work primitive usage outside the Builder Kit build flow

cases.description: When verify produces a FAIL verdict, the agent must record the FAIL script evidence artifact before routing back. The failure trace must not be discarded. Code-fix failures route to plan; fundamental/design failures route to design-probe and require a user decision. See Builder Kit build flow step:verify.
guidance_must_include: If verdict=FAIL: record the FAIL artifact
agent_must_do: run deterministic scripts and record command, exit_code, output_summary, artifact_path, verdict for each
when verdict=FAIL: write the FAIL artifact before routing back — do not discard it
route to plan (code fix, no user decision) or design-probe (fundamental gap, user decision required)

cases.description: merge-ready runs twice: once locally before opening the PR (lint, tests, diff review), and once after CI completes (ci_run, pr_approved, rollback_plan). Each has its own gate evidence. See Builder Kit build flow steps:merge-ready and merge-ready-ci.
agent_must_do: run lint and test checks as ScriptEvidence before opening PR
review the diff and confirm scope matches plan before recording verdict
not open the PR until local merge-ready verdict is READY
run merge-ready-ci as a separate step after CI completes — not skip it
